title: How to get a police certificate – Trinidad and Tobago - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate – Trinidad and Tobago
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Certificate of Character

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for:
- some temporary residence programs
Check your application package or instructions for details.
Apply online on the Trinidad and Tobago Police Service website:
- While completing the Certificate of Character Request Form, applicants will be able to choose the date of their appointment and the preferred police station they wish to visit to have their fingerprints taken.
- Applicants will receive a receipt with a unique tracking number upon submitting the online request.
- The applicant is responsible for printing their own application form on legal size paper and bringing it to the police station on the day of their appointment.
- The Certificate of Character processing fee is TT$50, which is to be paid at the police station on the day of the appointment.
Foreign residents will be required to follow these steps:
- Have their fingerprints recorded at a police station in whatever country they reside. If the fingerprints are not taken in a police station, have the fingerprint slip certified by an authorized notary public and have the notary public’s stamp affixed.
- If the fingerprints are not taken in a police station, have the fingerprint slip certified by an authorized notary public and have the notary public’s stamp affixed.
- Provide a copy of their passport showing the applicant ’s photograph, passport number, date of birth and signature.
- Include a money order equivalent to fifty TT dollars (TT$50.00) and the cost of return postage.
- Send the above documents by registered mail to: Office of the Senior Superintendent Criminal Investigations Department and Criminal Records Office Police Headquarters Cor. St. Vincent and Sackville Streets Port of Spain Trinidad and Tobago West Indies (Please send registered mail to the above office and a ddress ONLY.)
Send the above documents by registered mail to:
Office of the Senior Superintendent Criminal Investigations Department and Criminal Records Office Police Headquarters Cor. St. Vincent and Sackville Streets Port of Spain Trinidad and Tobago West Indies (Please send registered mail to the above office and a ddress ONLY.)
The Office of the Senior Superintendent CID/CRO will send the completed Certificate of Character back to the original sender by registered mail within three (3) days of receiving the documents.
Consult the Trinidad and Tobago Police Service website for instructions.
Trinidad and Tobago Police Service Criminal Investigations Department and Criminal Records Office Police Headquarters Cor. St. Vincent and Sackville Streets Port-of-Spain Telephone: 868-627-3761
